gpu: ANGLE (Intel, Intel(R) Arc(TM) 140V GPU (16GB) (0x000064A0) Direct3D11 vs_5_0 ps_5_0, D3D11)

analysis.summary: GPU raycasting via Three.js Raycaster is ~1.3x faster than CPU detailed ray-trace but with 30% precision loss. The bottleneck is the per-point Raycaster lookup (90% of GPU time), not the shadow map rendering itself.
analysis.cpuBreakdown: CPU: 35.8s entirely in polygon intersection with BVH acceleration
analysis.gpuBreakdown: GPU: mesh load 0.2s + shadow map renders 2.6s (160ms/render) + raycaster lookups 25.4s (1270 µs/point/instant)
analysis.precisionNote: The 30% mismatch is due to geometric differences: CPU uses precise polygon-ray intersection while GPU uses extruded Shape geometry. The CPU also applies a building-height bias and checks from exact terrain elevation.
analysis.recommendation: A true GPU shadow-map approach (reading depth buffer instead of CPU raycasting) would eliminate the 25s lookup bottleneck. Expected speedup: ~15-20x (mesh load 0.2s + renders 2.6s = 2.8s vs CPU 35.8s). However, precision would require careful shadow map setup and bias tuning.
